findings.message: package.json bumps @angular/* to ^21.0.0 but package-lock.json still pins the 18.2.14 tree, leaving the lockfile inconsistent with the manifest. A real `npm ci` would fail or resolve a mismatched tree. The implementer documented that regenerating it requires registry access blocked by the sandbox. Non-blocking here because all verifiable acceptance criteria (AC-1..AC-5) are backend-contract Jest tests that pass and don't depend on the lockfile, but this must be regenerated where npm has network before merge/deploy.

The supertest e2e spec the ACs are literally phrased against is not run by `npm test` (lives outside the `src` rootDir and supertest isn't installed in this skeleton) — consistent with the existing priorities.e2e-spec.ts. The same HTTP-status/payload semantics are verified in-process by tasks.controller.spec.ts through the real controller + a ValidationPipe matching main.ts, so each AC is genuinely exercised by the standard toolchain. Wire up supertest + a jest-e2e config in CI to run the wire-level version.

The official `ng update` migrations could not be run because no @angular/cli/devkit exists in this jest-only skeleton. The manual template/state migration achieves the equivalent (control-flow blocks, OnPush, signals) and is complete.
